theorems.title: OASG Existence
theorems.scope_note: Defended as the battery witness-row OASG existence theorem under explicit arbitrage reachability and controller-fault independence assumptions.
theorems.weakest_step: Boundary reachability remains domain-specific and is discharged here by explicit arbitrage reachability rather than by a generic dispatch argument.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: Runtime and benchmark code expose observed-vs-true disagreement directly, but the arbitrage reachability step remains a theorem-level assumption rather than a runtime proof object.
theorems.remediation_detail: Keep A11 explicit and battery-scoped unless a stronger dispatch-model derivation is written.

code_anchors.note: Exposes true and observed trajectories on the battery witness row.

theorems.title: Safety Preservation
theorems.scope_note: Defended as a one-step true-state postcondition whose tightened margin already absorbs the one-step model-error allowance.
theorems.weakest_step: The absorbed tightening must match the runtime FTIT/shield implementation exactly; any separation between theory margin and repair margin breaks the V3 story.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The battery tightening, repair, guarantee check, and runtime assertion surface all carry the absorbed margin explicitly.
theorems.remediation_detail: Keep the absorbed margin explicit in theorem text, repair metadata, and true-state invariance checks.

code_anchors.note: Checks the true-state one-step postcondition with model-error slack.
Enforces the runtime theorem hook for one-step safety.

theorems.title: ORIUS Core Envelope Derivation
theorems.scope_note: Defended as the per-step envelope derivation under the explicit battery assumptions and the narrowed reliability-score interpretation.
theorems.weakest_step: The theorem is only as good as the runtime-to-risk bridge; the repo now keeps that bridge explicit and battery-scoped instead of hiding it inside generic conformal language.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The runtime now emits an explicit T3a theorem-contract summary that separates executable envelope checks from the declared battery calibration bridge.
theorems.remediation_detail: Keep the reliability-score disclaimer explicit and avoid reintroducing weighted-exchangeability overclaims.

code_anchors.note: Attaches theorem-contract summaries to the typed runtime result.
Canonical theorem-contract summary for the narrowed T3a surface.

theorems.title: ORIUS Core Aggregation Corollary
theorems.scope_note: Derived corollary that converts the predictable per-step budget into the episode-average envelope.
theorems.weakest_step: This row is bookkeeping rather than new theory; the actual burden sits in T3a.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The corollary is exactly the aggregation performed by the episode envelope helper.
theorems.remediation_detail: Keep T3b corollary-only and avoid collapsing it back into a single overloaded T3 row.

test_anchors.note: Audit regression for the T3 split.

theorems.title: Observation Necessity / No Free Safety
theorems.scope_note: Defended as the observation-necessity witness for the fixed-margin quality-ignorant controller class on the battery row with explicit arbitrage reachability.
theorems.weakest_step: The constructive witness is intentionally class-scoped; widening the controller class would require a new proof.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The repo contains the necessary baseline and witness pieces, but the theorem remains constructive rather than fully executable.
theorems.remediation_detail: Preserve the fixed-margin quality-ignorant controller definition and avoid broadening the theorem into a claim that every ambiguity class is unsafe.

theorems.title: Certificate Validity Horizon Definition
theorems.scope_note: Kept as the constructive maximal forward-tube containment definition and excluded from defended theorem counts.
theorems.weakest_step: This row is intentionally definitional; future-step probability language is kept out of the defended theorem count.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The helper computes the maximal deterministic containment horizon directly.
theorems.remediation_detail: Do not promote T5 back into defended headline theorem counts without a separate temporal concentration proof.

theorems.title: Certificate Expiration Bound
theorems.scope_note: Defended as the confidence-aware closed-form battery expiration theorem with explicit delta dependence and first-passage side conditions.
theorems.weakest_step: The defended row depends on keeping the delta-aware helper canonical and not silently widening the theorem surface back to legacy no-delta semantics.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The exported helper carries the closed-form first-passage formula, explicit side conditions, and fail-closed theorem metadata that forbids legacy no-delta semantics.
theorems.remediation_detail: Keep the no-delta legacy semantics out of the theorem-facing API and preserve the explicit first-passage side conditions.

theorems.title: Feasible Fallback Existence
theorems.scope_note: Defended as a battery-specific piecewise fallback theorem: safe hold on the interior, safe landing near the boundary, and fail-closed infeasibility otherwise.
theorems.weakest_step: The theorem is battery-specific and only defended for the piecewise hold-or-safe-landing fallback surface implemented by the runtime.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The validator now certifies the piecewise T7 surface directly: interior hold, boundary recovery by safe landing, and fail-closed infeasibility.
theorems.remediation_detail: Preserve the piecewise hold-or-safe-landing scope and do not generalize T7 into a cross-domain transfer theorem.

theorems.title: Graceful Degradation Dominance
theorems.scope_note: Supporting comparison theorem at the sequence level; the strict absorbing-landing claim is not part of the defended headline core.
theorems.weakest_step: The helper proves comparison for supplied paired sequences under a shared fault trace, not a full absorbing-landing dynamics theorem.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The executable helper matches the narrowed statement exactly.
theorems.remediation_detail: Keep strict improvement phrasing tied to supplied paired sequences under the same admissible fault trace.

theorems.title: Universal Impossibility, T9
theorems.scope_note: Visible only as a scoped impossibility extension under the explicit geometric-mixing assumption A10b.
theorems.weakest_step: The witness constant is still domain-scoped rather than universally instantiated.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The helper exposes the algebra once the witness constant is provided.
theorems.remediation_detail: Keep T9 out of defended headline counts until the witness constant and mixing bridge are fully discharged.

theorems.title: Boundary-indistinguishability lower bound, T10
theorems.scope_note: Visible only as a scoped lower-bound frontier under the explicit TV bridge A13 and boundary-mass assumptions.
unresolved_assumptions: Unsafe-side boundary-mass sequence p_t is supplied explicitly.
theorems.weakest_step: The theorem stays scoped because the unsafe-side error bridge is not promoted beyond the explicit boundary-mass model.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The helper implements the stylized lower-bound algebra without overstating it as a universal converse.
theorems.remediation_detail: Preserve the explicit A13 + p_t boundary assumptions in all theorem-facing surfaces.

theorems.title: Typed structural transfer theorem, T11
theorems.scope_note: Defended as the forward four-obligation one-step transfer theorem; the converse remains a separate structural failure proposition.
typed_obligations: Coverage obligation for the observation-consistent state set.
Soundness of the tightened safe-action set.
Repair membership in the tightened safe-action set.
Fallback admissibility when the tightened set is empty.
dependencies: {'Proposition': 'Failure of any transfer obligation breaks the reference proof pattern'}
theorems.weakest_step: The theorem is forward-only at the active surface; stronger episode claims require an explicit domain risk budget.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The authoritative typed contract now emits a four-obligation theorem-contract summary over runtime artifacts; the five-invariant mini-harness remains supporting-only.
theorems.remediation_detail: Do not blur the supporting mini-harness into the active T11 transfer theorem.

code_anchors.note: Four-obligation theorem-contract summary over runtime artifacts.

theorems.title: T10_T11_ObservationAmbiguitySandwich: Covered Observation-Ambiguity Optimality
theorems.scope_note: Supporting optimality corollary under covered observation ambiguity; it does not assert unrestricted global optimality for all physical AI systems.
typed_obligations: Observation ambiguity classes are explicit.
The common safe core is computed as the intersection of state-conditioned safe action sets.
ORIUS releases only actions safe for every state in its covered uncertainty set.
Probabilistic coverage is reported as an alpha-bound rather than unconditional zero violation.
theorems.weakest_step: The lower bound is a Bayes ambiguity-risk bound, not the false claim that differing safe sets alone imply unavoidable violation.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The executable witness computes common safe cores, observation-only Bayes lower bounds, and covered ORIUS release upper bounds with explicit alpha-bound semantics.
theorems.remediation_detail: Keep the theorem phrased as safety-optimal under covered observation ambiguity, not as a global optimality theorem for every physical-AI system.

code_anchors.note: Publication-facing executable witness for the lower/upper sandwich.
Computes the observation-only Bayes lower bound.
Certifies the covered ORIUS upper-bound side.

test_anchors.note: Guards against the false unsafe-ambiguity overclaim.

theorems.title: T11_AV_BrakeHold: AV Brake-Hold Runtime Lemma
theorems.scope_note: Supporting AV runtime lemma under forward-only T11; bounded to promoted ORIUS replay rows and the longitudinal brake-hold postcondition.
typed_obligations: T11 coverage obligation is runtime-linked.
T11 sound safe-action set obligation is runtime-linked.
T11 repair-membership obligation is runtime-linked.
T11 fallback-admissibility obligation is runtime-linked.
True brake-hold runtime postcondition passes for the AV replay row.
theorems.weakest_step: Row-local replay witness only; it does not prove complete autonomous-driving closure.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: Runtime traces emit T11 status, certificate validity, and the AV brake-hold postcondition into domain contract witnesses.
theorems.remediation_detail: Missing T11 status, failed obligations, invalid certificate, or false postcondition must make the witness fail.

code_anchors.note: Emits AV T11 and brake-hold witness fields.

theorems.title: T11_HC_FailSafeRelease: Healthcare Fail-Safe Release Runtime Lemma
theorems.scope_note: Supporting healthcare runtime lemma under forward-only T11; bounded to promoted MIMIC monitoring rows and the fail-safe alert-release postcondition.
typed_obligations: T11 coverage obligation is runtime-linked.
T11 sound safe-action set obligation is runtime-linked.
T11 repair-membership obligation is runtime-linked.
T11 fallback-admissibility obligation is runtime-linked.
Healthcare true fail-safe alert-release runtime postcondition passes.
theorems.weakest_step: Row-local monitoring witness only; it does not prove regulated clinical deployment closure.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: Runtime traces emit T11 status, certificate validity, and the healthcare fail-safe release postcondition into domain contract witnesses.
theorems.remediation_detail: Missing T11 status, failed obligations, invalid certificate, or false postcondition must make the witness fail.

code_anchors.note: Emits healthcare T11 and fail-safe witness fields.

theorems.title: T6_AV_FallbackValidity: AV One-Step Fallback Certificate Lemma
theorems.scope_note: Supporting AV certificate-validity lemma under T6/T11; degraded full-brake fallback releases are one-step valid only.
typed_obligations: T6 first-passage validity semantics for positive-margin hold certificates.
T11 runtime witness is linked.
Runtime witness: AV full-brake fallback action is emitted for degraded fallback rows.
Runtime witness: AV true brake-hold postcondition passes.
theorems.weakest_step: One-step fallback validity only; degraded observation never grants multi-step validity.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: Shared domain validity helper assigns H_t=1 only to AV fail-safe fallback certificates.
theorems.remediation_detail: Non-fail-safe fallback or failed postcondition must invalidate the witness.

code_anchors.note: Bounded fallback and T6-style hold validity helper.

theorems.title: T6_HC_FallbackValidity: Healthcare One-Step Fallback Certificate Lemma
theorems.scope_note: Supporting healthcare certificate-validity lemma under T6/T11; degraded max-alert fallback releases are one-step valid only.
typed_obligations: T6 first-passage validity semantics for positive-margin hold certificates.
T11 runtime witness is linked.
Healthcare max-alert fallback action is emitted for degraded fallback rows.
Healthcare true fail-safe alert-release postcondition passes.
theorems.weakest_step: One-step fallback validity only; degraded observation never grants multi-step validity.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: Shared domain validity helper assigns H_t=1 only to healthcare max-alert fallback certificates.
theorems.remediation_detail: Non-fail-safe fallback or failed postcondition must invalidate the witness.

code_anchors.note: Bounded fallback and T6-style hold validity helper.

theorems.title: T_EQ_Battery_RuntimeArtifactPackage: Battery Equal Artifact Discipline Package
theorems.scope_note: Supporting artifact-discipline row; it checks battery theorem, runtime, comparator, ablation, negative-control, utility, and reproducibility evidence without changing the flagship theorem tier.
typed_obligations: Battery locked witness runtime trace exists.
Battery comparator, ablation, and negative-control rows are runtime-denominator rows.
Battery ORIUS useful work exceeds the degenerate safe-fallback comparator.
theorems.weakest_step: Artifact-discipline equivalence only; it does not add a new battery theorem.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: Equal-domain builder reads battery runtime traces and locked witness surfaces into strict per-domain artifact gates.
theorems.remediation_detail: Missing runtime-native comparator, ablation, negative-control, utility, or reproducibility evidence fails the equal-domain gate.

theorems.title: T_EQ_AV_RuntimeArtifactPackage: AV Equal Artifact Discipline Package
theorems.scope_note: Supporting artifact-discipline row for the bounded AV replay contract; it does not assert full autonomous-driving field closure.
typed_obligations: Vehicle T11 and T6 runtime lemmas are linked.
Vehicle comparator, ablation, and negative-control rows are runtime-denominator rows.
Vehicle ORIUS useful work exceeds always-brake useful work.
theorems.weakest_step: Artifact-discipline equivalence only; it remains bounded to the promoted AV replay contract.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: AV runtime generation emits runtime-native comparator artifacts consumed by the equal-domain gate.
theorems.remediation_detail: Missing runtime-native comparator, ablation, negative-control, utility, or reproducibility evidence fails the equal-domain gate.

code_anchors.note: Emits AV runtime and comparator artifacts.

theorems.title: T11_Byzantine: Byzantine Trimmed-Mean Bound
theorems.scope_note: Supporting bounded Byzantine robustness theorem under the stated trimmed-mean assumptions.
typed_obligations: Byzantine fraction f is strictly below 1/3.
Trim fraction beta is at least f.
Tail-contamination model places adversarial readings in the trimmed tails.
theorems.weakest_step: Simplified bounded-fraction model.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: Helper matches appendix scope.
theorems.remediation_detail: Keep bounded-fraction scope explicit.

theorems.weakest_step: Design schedule rather than physical sensing law.

theorems.scope_note: Visible only as a stylized minimax extension.

theorems.title: T_trajectory_PAC: PAC Trajectory Certificate
theorems.scope_note: Defended as the implemented Bonferroni/union-bound trajectory certificate and nothing stronger.
typed_obligations: Bonferroni/union-bound aggregation over the horizon.
theorems.weakest_step: The certificate is conservative by design because it stacks finite-sample and worst-case reliability corrections.
theorems.code_correspondence_detail: The helper, tests, and appendix all use the same union-bound semantics.
theorems.remediation_detail: Any future martingale strengthening must appear as a new theorem, not as a silent replacement.

namespace_drift.issue: Legacy auxiliary coverage helpers previously reused active T9/T10 numbering.
namespace_drift.impact: The code namespace could silently contradict the manuscript theorem namespace.
namespace_drift.remediation: Keep those helpers explicitly auxiliary and route theorem-facing claims through the YAML registry.

namespace_drift.issue: The five-invariant reference harness was described too broadly relative to active T11.
namespace_drift.impact: Supporting adapter checks could be mistaken for the full transfer theorem.
namespace_drift.remediation: Treat the mini-harness as supporting evidence only; active T11 flows through the typed four-obligation surface.

namespace_drift.issue: The legacy register mixed raw inventory and defended-surface interpretation.
namespace_drift.impact: Downstream tooling could select broader or legacy rows by accident.
namespace_drift.remediation: Keep the register as YAML-generated inventory; use active_theorem_audit.* as the reconciled defended surface.
